mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is in a sustained upward trend, higher short rates raise equity discount rates and tighten financing conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 4.99 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the FEDFUNDS signal has Rank IC greater than -0.03 in absolute failure direction over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in RSAFS signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend persists.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 363806 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is not associated with higher 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, defined as Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a weakening residential investment cycle that reduces construction activity, consumer durable demand, and credit appetite, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1957.0 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if conditional mean returns are not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates expanding real output and operating leverage for cyclicals, so a positive 3-month industrial production trend drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 112.0991 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum produces a 20-day forward return hit rate below 50% and a Rank IC below 0.03 across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is already high, inflation pressure forces investors to price additional tightening and lower valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 201.9 and FEDFUNDS was 4.99 in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-high trigger does not underperform the unconditional 5-day S&P 500 return, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a low UNRATE signals tight labor markets that support household income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days unless wage-pressure tightening dominates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE is below 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 135230.0 and UNRATE was 4.6 in the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and UNRATE below 5.0% is followed by below-median 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, restrictive policy is hitting real demand with a lag, reducing expected earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth slows and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11403.6 as of the latest available quarterly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances do not have lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered instances, or if the GDPC1 slowdown and FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE from a low starting point signals that labor demand has begun to weaken, so a rising unemployment trend predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.6 as of the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: transition from expansion to labor-market slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average return.
